notes: Integrated from the real Grade 2 Quarter 3, Book 18 source file.
Advanced card: longest texts and/or more complex sentence structure.

book.title: Puss in Boots
book.description: Puss in Boots includes a six-chapter reading sequence, vocabulary, comprehension questions, journal/writing prompts, and assessment checks.

choiceGuidance.reason: longest texts and/or more complex sentence structure

weeks.title: Puss in Boots Book Card
reading: Chapter 4: An Audience with the King
vocabulary: 1. acumen - the ability to make good judgments and quick decisions
2. lamented - expressed sorrow or regret about something
1. articulate - expressing ideas clearly and effectively
1. etiquette - the rules of polite and proper behavior
2. benefactor - a person who gives help or money to others
1. audacious - showing willingness to take bold risks
1. aristocrat - a member of the highest social class
journal: Write about how Puss uses clever thinking to help his friend.
Explain whether Puss is a hero. Use details from the story.
writing: Write about how Puss uses clever thinking to help his friend.
Explain whether Puss is a hero. Use details from the story.
questions: 1. What did the eldest son inherit from his father?
2. How did Jack feel about his inheritance?
1. What amazing ability did the cat suddenly reveal?
2. What did the cat request from Jack to carry out his plan?
1. How did the cat catch a rabbit?
2. Where did the cat take the rabbit instead of bringing it to Jack?
1. What title did the cat claim his master held?
2. How did the king respond to the cat's visits and gifts?
1. What did the cat learn about the king's plans?
2. What emergency did the cat pretend was happening?
1. How did the king respond to the emergency?
2. What was the result of the cat's elaborate scheme?
assessment: Use chapter comprehension questions for daily checks.
Use journal/writing prompt as written-response evidence.
